node.statement: (Dimension consequence). Since Φ is an affine isomorphism between F_{S(A)}(φ) and the set D = {T ∈ π_φ(A)'^+ : ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1}, we have dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim(D). The set D is a base (cross-section) of the cone π_φ(A)'^+ cut by the hyperplane ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1 in the real vector space π_φ(A)'^{sa}. Since ⟨·ξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ is a single real-linear constraint on π_φ(A)'^{sa}, we have dim(D) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1 (the -1 is the codimension of the normalisation hyperplane within the affine span of D). This requires that ξ_φ is a separating vector for π_φ(A)', or at least that the functional T ↦ ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ is faithful on π_φ(A)'^+ (which follows from ξ_φ being cyclic for π_φ(A), hence separating for π_φ(A)' by Tomita-Takesaki theory, when π_φ(A)'' = π_φ(A)̄^{WOT}).
